items.title: Di era AI, yang menang bukan yang kerja paling banyak
items.description:
Saya mulai dari pertanyaan yang kelihatannya sederhana: mana yang lebih kuat, motivasi, disiplin, konsistensi, atau obsesi? Saya kira jawabannya salah satu dari empat itu. Ternyata bukan. Setelah saya pikir lebih jauh, pertanyaannya sendiri yang salah arah.

Keempatnya bukan puncak apa-apa.

items.title: Building a cross-user review graph with pgvector on Amazon Aurora
items.description: Every review app is a silo. Yelp reviews places, Amazon reviews its own catalog, Letterboxd reviews film. I wanted to build the opposite: one place where you can log and rate anything — a ball-point pen, a burger, a cruise — and have your review pool together with everyone else's

items.title: Serving cheap when two models agree: a measured cost lever
items.description:
The problem

A cost efficient AI system sends easy work to a cheap model and only escalates hard work to an expensive frontier model. The trouble is knowing which is which. When a task has a test, like code with unit tests, you just run the test: if the cheap answer passes,

items.title: The Unicode trap I had to design around in a LinkedIn formatter
items.description:
I built a small tool for formatting LinkedIn posts, and the deceptively simple part was also the most important part:

styled text is not formatting. It is Unicode substitution.

That sounds obvious once you say it out loud, but it changes the product decision.
